channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Middle East latest: Palestinian militants name 3 Israeli men to be freed in next hostage release
item.description: The families of Israeli hostages still held by the Hamas militant group on Friday welcomed the “joyous news” that three men, all taken from the same kibbutz on Oct. 7, 2023, are expected to be freed on Saturday in exchange for more than 300 Palestinian prisoners held in Israeli jails.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: A drone damaged the outer shell of Ukraine's Chernobyl nuclear plant. Radiation levels are normal
item.description: CHERNOBYL NUCLEAR POWER STATION, Ukraine (AP) — A drone armed with a warhead hit the protective outer shell of Ukraine's Chernobyl nuclear plant early Friday, damaging the structure and briefly starting a fire, in an attack Kyiv blamed on Russia. The Kremlin denied it was responsible.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV and EFREM LUKATSKY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Hegseth says he and Vance are 'on the same page' despite VP's remark on US troops in Ukraine
item.description: Defense Secretary Hegseth was asked on Friday about the possibility of the U.S. sending troops to Ukraine just after Vice President Vance said the option had not been ruled out.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Rwanda-backed rebels claim to have seized a second airport in east Congo
item.description: GOMA, Congo (AP) — Rwanda-backed rebels in eastern Congo claimed Friday that they have seized a second airport in the region following a days-long advance, as the U.N. warned that the recent escalation of fighting with government forces has left 350,000 internally displaced people without shelter.
item.dc:creator: CHINEDU ASADU and JUSTIN KABUMBA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Europe quietly works on a plan to send troops to Ukraine for post-war security
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — Increasingly alarmed that U.S. security priorities lie elsewhere, a group of European countries has been quietly working on a plan to send troops into Ukraine to help enforce any future peace settlement with Russia.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Hostages freed from Gaza painfully piece together a changed world
item.description: After nearly 500 days in captivity in Gaza, with little access to media, recently freed hostages have many questions.
item.dc:creator: Tia Goldenberg, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Prosecutors see an Islamic extremist motive in the Munich car-ramming attack
item.description: MUNICH (AP) — The suspect in the car-ramming attack in Munich that left more than 30 people injured appears to have had an Islamic extremist motive, but there's no evidence that he was involved with any radical network, authorities said Friday.
item.dc:creator: PHILIPP JENNE and GEIR MOULSON, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Guarded optimism in India as Trump and Modi outline plans to deepen defense partnership
item.description: There is a guarded optimism among military experts in India as Prime Minister Narendra Modi and U.S. President Donald Trump have outlined plans to ramp up sales of defense systems to New Delhi to deepen the U.S.-India strategic relationship.
item.dc:creator: Aijaz Hussain, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: A driver plows into a demonstration in Munich, injuring 30 people
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — A driver drove a car into a labor union demonstration in central Munich on Thursday, injuring 30 people including children, authorities said. Officials said it was believed to be an attack. The suspect, an Afghan asylum-seeker, was arrested.
item.dc:creator: GEIR MOULSON, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump’s Russia-Ukraine moves unnerve Senate GOP
item.description: President Trump’s first major moves toward peace negotiations between Russia and Ukraine are unsettling Republicans in the Senate who want to make sure Russian President Vladimir Putin faces consequences for launching an unprovoked invasion into his European neighbor.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Russia says US 'our main counterpart' in peace talks, sidelining Ukraine
item.description: Russia said Thursday that the United States will be its “main counterpart” in forthcoming peace talks to end its invasion of Ukraine, sidelining Kyiv in the negotiations.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Trump says he wants Russia back in the G7
item.description: President Trump said Thursday he would like to see Russia reinstated in the Group of Seven (G7) major economies and suggested various actors other than Moscow were to blame for Russia's invasion of Ukraine in 2022.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Russian-American ballerina 'in transit' after US says she was wrongfully detained, boyfriend says
item.description: Russian-American ballerina Ksenia Karelina, who was imprisoned in Russia after donating just over $50 to a Ukrainian charity last year, is “in transit," her boyfriend tells Fox News.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NATO allies insist Ukraine and Europe must be in peace talks as Trump touts Putin meeting
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — Several NATO allies stressed on Thursday that Ukraine and Europe must not be cut out of any peace negotiations as U.S. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth denied that the United States is betraying the war-ravaged country.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Saudi educator known for charity and prisoner work wins $1 million Global Teacher Prize
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — A Saudi educator known for his charity work and instructing prisoners won the $1 million Global Teacher Prize on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL and MALAK HARB, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: O'Reilly predicts Trump and Putin will 'get a ceasefire' in Ukraine
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy "has no power" in opposing a ceasefire deal, according to O'Reilly.
item.dc:creator: Chris Cuomo, NewsNation

item.title: Nearly everyone in the world breathes bad air. How to lower your risk
item.description: Polluted air, laden noxious gasses or tiny, invisible particles that burrow into human bodies, kills 7 million people prematurely every year, the U.N. health agency estimates.
item.dc:creator: ANIRUDDHA GHOSAL, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Rubio says American Marc Fogel freed from Russia due to 'strength' of Trump
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio said Tuesday that Marc Fogel, an American who had been detained in Russia since 2021, was freed because the U.S. has a "strong president.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hamas' threat to delay hostage release raises fears for ceasefire
item.description: The next handover of three hostages had been scheduled for Saturday, and families say time is running out for those still alive.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's AI ambition and China's DeepSeek overshadow an AI summit in Paris
item.description: PARIS (AP) — The geopolitics of artificial intelligence will be in focus at a major summit in France where world leaders, executives and experts will hammer out pledges on guiding the development of the rapidly advancing technology.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET and KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Mother, son die from food poisoning at Dominican Republic resort
item.description: A grieving family filed a lawsuit after a mother and son died from food poisoning while vacationing at a Dominican Republic resort.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Unger, NewsNation
